data.title: The Central Bank of Russia plans to mandate real-name registration for cryptocurrency transactions and comprehensively strengthen the supervision of fund flows.
data.description: The Central Bank of Russia announced plans to require all cryptocurrency traders to complete Know Your Customer (KYC) verification and restrict unregistered users from withdrawing cryptocurrency assets from domestic platforms, aiming to strengthen regulation of the domestic cryptocurrency market. According to the proposed rules, Russia will require domestic exchanges to fully implement real-name registration and prohibit users from transferring funds from domestic custodial wallets to overseas non-custodial wallets. Simultaneously, regulators plan to require residents to declare their overseas cryptocurrency holdings to enhance monitoring of capital outflows. Central Bank Deputy Chairman Vladimir Chistyukhin stated that the new regulations will not prohibit individuals from holding or using cryptocurrency assets, but the focus is on improving transparency and compliance to prevent money laundering risks. This policy is expected to be implemented in July this year along with a new round of cryptocurrency regulatory legislation. Analysts believe this move reflects Russia's attempt to control capital flows by strengthening cryptocurrency regulation amidst economic pressure. (DL News)

data.title: FOMC Member and Chicago Fed President Goolsbee to Join Panel Discussion at Semafor 2026 World Economic Conference
data.description: FOMC member and Chicago Federal Reserve President Austan Goolsbee is set to participate in a panel discussion at the Semafor 2026 World Economic Conference in ten minutes. According to Jin10, this event is expected to provide insights into the current economic landscape and monetary policy considerations. Goolsbee's participation comes as the global financial community closely monitors central bank actions and economic indicators.

data.title: CLARITY Act Faces Critical Two-Week Senate Window Amid Industry Concerns
data.description: The CLARITY Act is facing a crucial two-week period for Senate action, with industry advocates cautioning that a failure this spring could postpone U.S. crypto legislation until 2030. According to NS3.AI, the odds of the act passing this year have dropped to 58% from 82% in February, as indicated by Polymarket. The Senate Banking Committee has yet to schedule a markup, while banks and crypto firms remain divided over whether stablecoin issuers should pass yields to users.

data.title: U.S. Senate to Release Revised Draft on Stablecoin Yield Distribution
data.description:
The U.S. Senate is expected to unveil a revised draft concerning the distribution of stablecoin yields this week. According to ChainCatcher, the ongoing debate between banks and cryptocurrency firms over whether crypto exchanges should be allowed to pay yields to stablecoin holders through reward programs has stalled the legislative process of the Clarity Act.

A recent report from the White House Council of Economic Advisers suggests that prohibiting stablecoin yields would have minimal impact on small banks, potentially increasing bank lending by only 0.02%. However, the American Bankers Association argues that this analysis underestimates the associated risks. Observers warn that overly stringent regulations in the draft could drive users and liquidity to jurisdictions that permit yield payments.

data.title: Today, US Bitcoin ETFs saw a net outflow of 3,539 BTC, and Ethereum ETFs saw a net outflow of 780 ETH.
data.description: According to Lookonchain monitoring, today the US Bitcoin ETF saw a net outflow of 3,539 BTC, the Ethereum ETF saw a net outflow of 780 ETH, and the Solana ETF saw a net inflow of 2,469 SOL.

data.title: Scroll Faces Significant Fund Outflow Following Ether.fi Migration
data.description:
Ethereum Layer-2 network Scroll has experienced a substantial outflow of funds, with its total value locked (TVL) decreasing by approximately $160 million. According to ChainCatcher, this was triggered by the migration of the top decentralized application Ether.fi to the Optimism network. The annual fee loss is estimated at around $13 million.

In response, Scroll plans to propose the dissolution of its decentralized security council and reduce DAO members to cut operational costs. Control of the network will be transferred to an internal team. Following the protocol migration, Scroll temporarily increased network gas fees by 1,280 times, resulting in users paying over $50,000 in additional fees, although this has now returned to normal levels. After these adjustments, Scroll's TVL has dropped to approximately $23 million. The organization assures that all contract changes will remain transparently verifiable on-chain.

data.title: CoW DAO Alerts Users to Avoid CoW Swap Due to Frontend Issues
data.description: CoW DAO has issued a warning on the X platform regarding a problem with the CoW Swap frontend. According to Odaily, users are advised to refrain from using the service until further notice. The organization is working to resolve the issue and will provide updates as they become available.

data.title: Trump's stance wavered: initially ruling out Pakistan, then changing his mind, still favoring it as a negotiating ground for the US-Iran conflict.
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump said in a phone interview on Tuesday that the United States would not return to Pakistan for any future peace talks with Iran. This came after a marathon negotiation over the weekend failed to reach an agreement. “Things are happening, but a little slow,” he said. “We might choose another location; we have alternatives in mind.” When asked if Turkey was being considered, Trump said, “No, somewhere more central. Maybe Europe.” After a brief exchange with the reporter, Trump called again to add, “You should stay there. Something might happen in the next two days. We’re more inclined to go to Pakistan. Because that Field Marshal did a great job.” He was referring to Pakistani Field Marshal Asim Munir. Trump established contact with Munir during last year’s Indo-Pakistani war, which resulted in a peace agreement reached in just four days with U.S. mediation. “He was great, so we’re more likely to go back,” Trump said. “Why go to a country that has nothing to do with this?” (Jin Shi)

data.title: CoW DAO: There is an issue with the CoW Swap frontend; users are advised to temporarily refrain from using it.
data.description: CoW DAO posted on the X platform that there was a problem with the CoW Swap frontend and advised users not to use it for the time being.

data.title: Goldman Sachs Predicts Potential Rebound for Magnificent Seven Stocks
data.description: Goldman Sachs managing director Lee Coppersmith has expressed optimism about the potential rebound of the Magnificent Seven stocks, suggesting that the current market setup presents an attractive opportunity to increase exposure to broader U.S. equities. According to NS3.AI, Coppersmith drew parallels between the present market conditions and the March 2020 COVID-related downturn, indicating a favorable environment for investment.

data.title: Modi Discusses Iran Conflict with Trump Amid Strait of Hormuz Tensions
data.description: Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi announced that he had a conversation with U.S. President Donald Trump regarding the ongoing conflict in Iran and the escalating situation in the Strait of Hormuz. Bloomberg posted on X that the discussion focused on the implications of the conflict for global security and trade routes. The Strait of Hormuz, a critical passage for oil shipments, has been a focal point of international concern due to recent tensions. Modi emphasized the importance of maintaining stability in the region to ensure uninterrupted energy supplies and global economic stability. The dialogue between the two leaders underscores the significance of international cooperation in addressing geopolitical challenges.

data.title: Google DeepMind releases Gemini Robotics-ER 1.6
data.description: Google DeepMind released Gemini Robotics-ER 1.6, positioned as a high-level reasoning model for robots. Compared to its predecessors ER 1.5 and Gemini 3.0 Flash, it offers significant improvements in spatial reasoning and multi-view understanding. The model is available to developers through the Gemini API and Google AI Studio. Core upgrades include three key capabilities: 1. Improved pointing accuracy: Enables precise object detection, counting, spatial relationship reasoning (e.g., pointing out all objects that can fit into the blue cup), and motion trajectory planning, while correctly rejecting pointing to objects not present in the frame. 2. Successful multi-view detection: The robot can now integrate multiple camera feeds to determine task completion accuracy, even in occluded or dynamic environments. 3. New instrument reading capabilities: It can interpret various industrial instruments such as circular pressure gauges, vertical level indicators, and digital displays. Through agentic vision (visual reasoning + code execution), it performs step-by-step reasoning, first magnifying detailed areas, then calculating proportions and intervals through pointing and code, and finally combining this with world knowledge to derive the reading.

data.title: Kering Group Reports Gucci's Q1 Revenue Below Market Expectations
data.description: Kering Group announced that Gucci's revenue for the first quarter was 1.35 billion euros, falling short of the market's general expectation of 1.37 billion euros. According to Jin10, this performance highlights a discrepancy between anticipated and actual financial outcomes for the luxury brand.

data.title: Trump: Iran talks "may" take place in Pakistan within the next two days
data.description: US President Trump told the New York Post that negotiations on Iran "may" be held in Pakistan "within the next two days," adding that the US "prefers" holding the negotiations in Pakistan. (Jinshi)

data.title: Ethereum Foundation Launches Audit Subsidy Program to Enhance Ecosystem Security
data.description:
The Ethereum Foundation has announced the launch of the 'Ethereum Audit Subsidy Program' on the X platform, aimed at providing financial support for security audits to developers. According to Odaily, this initiative is part of the EF's 'Trillion Dollar Security Initiative' and involves collaboration with several auditing and infrastructure organizations, including Nethermind, Chainlink Labs, and Areta, to evaluate and review project applications.

The Ethereum Foundation highlighted that while security audits are considered best practices in the industry, they often come with high costs. This program seeks to lower the entry barriers for developers, thereby supporting more innovative use cases and projects within the Ethereum ecosystem.

data.title: Cryptocurrency Loss Levels Highlight Market Trends
data.description: Glassnode has reported on the current state of relative unrealized losses in the cryptocurrency market. According to Foresight News, this metric measures the total unrealized losses as a percentage of market capitalization, indicating the depth of 'paper losses' among holders. As of now, Solana (SOL) stands at 54.8%, Bitcoin (BTC) at 11.9%, and Ethereum (ETH) at 16.6%. The elevated loss levels in altcoins suggest significant holdings at market peaks, with supply concentrated among buyers who entered near cycle highs.

data.title: U.S. Enforces Blockade on Iranian Ports
data.description: More than 10,000 U.S. personnel are currently enforcing a blockade on vessels entering and exiting Iranian ports, according to U.S. Central Command. In the first 24 hours, no vessels breached the blockade. Additionally, six merchant ships complied with U.S. military instructions.

data.title: France Lowers 2026 Economic Growth Forecast to 0.9%
data.description: France has revised its economic growth forecast for 2026, reducing it to 0.9%, according to a report by Agence France-Presse. This adjustment reflects a more cautious outlook for the country's economic performance in the coming years. The decision to lower the growth expectation comes amid various economic challenges and uncertainties that France is currently facing. The revised forecast indicates a need for strategic adjustments to address potential economic hurdles and ensure sustainable growth.

data.title: The Ethereum Foundation launches an audit subsidy program to reduce security costs for developers.
data.description: The Ethereum Foundation (ETH Zurich) announced the "Ethereum Audit Subsidy Program" on the X platform, partnering with multiple auditing and infrastructure organizations to provide subsidies for security audit fees for developers, aiming to improve the overall security of the ecosystem. Led by EF's "Trillion Dollar Security Initiative" and in collaboration with Nethermind, Chainlink Labs, and Areta, the program evaluates and reviews applications. The ETH Zurich stated that while security audits are industry best practices, they are costly; this initiative aims to lower the development barrier and support more innovative use cases and projects in the Ethereum ecosystem.

data.title: FluidStack Seeks $1 Billion Funding at $18 Billion Valuation
data.description: AI cloud platform FluidStack is reportedly aiming to raise approximately $1 billion at a valuation of $18 billion. According to Odaily, sources indicate that Leopold Aschenbrenner's firm, Situational Awareness, is in discussions to participate and co-lead the investment round. The funds are expected to accelerate FluidStack's expansion in AI computing and cloud infrastructure. FluidStack has previously established partnerships with several Bitcoin mining companies, including TeraWulf, Cipher Mining, and Hut 8.
